purpose: Teach Live News writing systems to produce clear, source-respectful public writing with durable rubrics instead of scattered templates.
courseConceptPolicy: Use communication and writing concepts as short original rubrics only. Do not copy course materials, lectures, textbooks, slides, or private student work into the repo.

rubrics.name: English Writing Clarity
rubrics.teacherUse: Check that each sentence has a clear subject, action, and object when possible.
rubrics.publicRule: Prefer plain words, clean grammar, and specific nouns over vague filler.

rubrics.name: Audience Adaptation
rubrics.teacherUse: Ask what a general reader needs first: event, person, place, consequence, or source.
rubrics.publicRule: Write for readers who may be scanning quickly and may not know the background.

rubrics.name: Rhetorical Situation
rubrics.teacherUse: Match purpose, audience, context, and channel before choosing a title, summary, SEO line, or social caption.
rubrics.publicRule: Do not make a caption sound like a headline, and do not make SEO text sound like a social hook.

rubrics.name: Public Speaking Structure
rubrics.teacherUse: Use an opening idea, one supporting detail, and a clean close when longer captions or deks need shape.
rubrics.publicRule: Lead with the point, support it with a confirmed detail, then send the reader to the exact Live News page when appropriate.

rubrics.name: Digital Media Scannability
rubrics.teacherUse: Check whether text works on small screens, cards, search snippets, and social previews.
rubrics.publicRule: Short surfaces need one strong idea, not a compressed paragraph.

rubrics.name: Intercultural Respect
rubrics.teacherUse: Look for stereotyping, loaded labels, assumptions about groups, and language that turns people into props.
rubrics.publicRule: Use neutral, respectful wording, especially for identity, nationality, religion, immigration, crime, conflict, and health stories.

rubrics.name: Evidence Integration
rubrics.teacherUse: Confirm that every public claim can be traced to title, summary, source metadata, approved story text, or trusted source research.
rubrics.publicRule: If the source context does not support a detail, do not publish that detail.

rubrics.name: Rhythm and Cadence
rubrics.teacherUse: Avoid repeated sentence starts, repeated lengths, and robotic card-to-card patterns.
rubrics.publicRule: Vary sentence rhythm while keeping the writing calm and clear.

rubrics.name: Multimodal Alignment
rubrics.teacherUse: Check that titles, summaries, card text, image plans, alt text, and captions all describe the same confirmed story.
rubrics.publicRule: The visual card should support the story, not create a different one.

lessonSequence.name: Gather confirmed context
lessonSequence.check: Build ArticleWritingContext and stop if the main event, source, confirmed facts, or exact story URL are missing.

lessonSequence.name: Choose the field purpose
lessonSequence.check: Decide whether the text is a title, description, dek, summary, SEO line, homepage card, social caption, or image/card text.

lessonSequence.name: Pick a writing shape
lessonSequence.check: Use a preferred Live News shape that matches the category and field.

lessonSequence.name: Write from facts
lessonSequence.check: Use confirmed article context only; never invent facts or public reaction.

lessonSequence.name: Run teacher checks
lessonSequence.check: Block weak fallback language, unsupported claims, copy risk, missing exact links, homepage links, and forced public safety framing.

lessonSequence.name: Select the strongest candidate
lessonSequence.check: Choose the highest-scoring candidate that passes story focus, fact faithfulness, specificity, clarity, rhythm, source respect, and digital readability.

teacherMemoryRule: Use editor-approved lessons and aggregate performance patterns as strategy only. Do not copy public comments, usernames, personal profiles, private messages, or creator wording.
